sources: Benzinga (news/WIIM) + Finnhub (analyst consensus) + SEC EDGAR (filings) + live prices + Jaleel Capital framework

macro.headline: Hawkish Fed, energy & geopolitics in the driver's seat
macro.body: Fed Chair Warsh scrapped forward guidance and the dot plot leans toward hikes, not cuts, so long-duration high-multiple growth (NOW, ADBE, NVDA, IOT) stays rate-sensitive. Energy and the Strait of Hormuz headlines are the live swing factor on inflation.

risk.detail: FY guide cut (Jun 4): Americas comps −5% (5th straight), tariffs gutted margins, China stalled.
FY26 growth cut 10.5→7–8.5%; Watchman/EP/urology soft; mgmt guides flat through Q3.
Beat & raised (Jun 11) yet sold off; AI-moat fear (Claude Design), CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis — the narrative, not the numbers, drives it.
China export-license tightening + Senate scrutiny; the memory/chip complex swings on AI-spending sentiment.
No bad news — strong Q1, IBM AI deal. But it's the biggest weight + a high multiple; in a hawkish-Fed software de-rate it leads the drawdown.
Trump MFN pricing deals (Gilead, Novartis signed) + import tariffs put a pricing-pressure overhang on the whole pharma cluster.
FY EPS guide cut on deal financing + a weak flu season; revenue raised. Defensive — a yellow flag, not a red one.
